MASTER JURY CALENDAR SET FOR MAY-15-2014 CONTINUED TO MASTER CALENDAR JURY ON MAY-19-2014 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 206 FOR TRIAL ASSIGNMENT. ALL PARTIES WERE PRESENT IN COURT. THE COURT WAS READY TO ASSIGN THE CASE TO JUDGE GARRETT WONG. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL, MR. BENETATOS, MADE A DISCLOSURE TO THE COURT THAT THEY TRIED A CASE TOGETHER, 15 YEARS AGO. DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL, MR. VARTAIN, ASKED THE COURT IF HE COULD TAKE A FEW MINUTES TO DECIDE. AFTER RECESS, MR. VARTAIN WOULD LIKE TO CHALLENGE JUDGE WONG FOR CAUSE BUT THEN THE COURT TOLD THE PARTIES THAT JUDGE WONG HAS RECUSED HIMSELF. JUDGE: CYNTHIA M. LEE; CLERK: R. VELUZ; COURT REPORTER: MARY ANN SCANLAN-STONE, CSR#8875 (415-834-1114).(206)

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Apr-08-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFFs VINCENZO CALOIARO and JOAN CALOIARO's MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUBPOENA OF MARCIA WALLACE IS OFF-CALENDAR PER THE REQUEST OF THE MOVING PARTY. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Marcia Anne Wallis And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-02-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: STEVEN MARTINI, ESQ., 415.488.5046, FOR PLAINTIFFS VINCENZO CALOIARO AND JOAN CALOIARO; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS SCOTT SIDWELL AND UNIVERSITY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Marcia Anne Wallis And For Sanctions is denied. Defendants have not shown good cause to depose Plaintiff's attorney. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $ 2,500.00 are awarded in favor of Plaintiff and against Defendants and their counsel to be paid within 30 days of the filing of the order. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR5976

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Marcia Anne Wallis And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-25-2014 Continued To Apr-02-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS SCOTT SIDWELL AND UNIVERSITY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO COMPEL THE DEPOSITION OF MARCIA ANNE WALLIS AND FOR SANCTIONS

Appearances: Michael J. Vartain of the Vartain Law Group (415.391.1155) for defendants and moving parties; Steven D. Martini of the Martini Law Group, PC (415.488.5046) for plaintiff and responding party.

Counsel do not stipulate to the matter being decided by Judge Pro Tem James Watson. Counsel present their arguments. The matter is continued to April 2, 2014 at 9:30 am to be heard by Judge Goldsmith. Judge Pro Tem: James Watson; Clerk: E. Larnauti; Not Reported (302/JPT)

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Pltf To Give His Deposition
Off Calendar May-31-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: Defendants' SCOTT SIDWELL, UNIVERSITY OF SAN FRANCISCO Motion to Compel Plaintiff CALOIARO's Deposition is DENIED as premature under C.C.P. 2025.450(a). Plaintiff has not failed to appear for the deposition date currently set for June 14, 2013. Prevailing party to prepare order. JUDGE PRO TEM REBECCA WOODSON, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Deft'S To Respond And Produce Documents Pursuant To Pltf'S Req. For Production And Inspection Of Documents And Things, Set Two
Off Calendar May-31-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. Plaintiff CALOIARO's Motion to Compel Defendants' to Respond and Produce Documents Pursuant to Plaintiff's RFPD, Set Two, is DENIED. Defendants timely responded to Plaintiff's RFPD, Set Two on April 26, 2013; Defendants subsequently produced documents on or about May 7, 2013. Prevailing party to prepare order. JUDGE PRO TEM REBECCA WOODSON, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.

Off Calendar Dec-18-2012 Continued To Mar-06-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendants Scott Sidwell And University Of San Francisco'S Demurrer To The 1st Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Nov-13-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MARCIA A. WALLIS OF THE WALLIS FIRM, TEL. NO. (650) 468-5556 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS;
ATTY. MICHAEL J. VARTARIN, TEL. NO. 391-1155 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendants Scott Sidwell And University Of San Francisco'S Demurrer To The 1st Amended Complaint is Sustained, with 20 days leave to amend as to all causes of action pertaining to Joan Caloiaro. CCP 430.10(e). No cause of action contains any allegations of injury as to Joan Caloiaro, even taking into consideration the incorporation of certain paragraphs by reference. Further, to the extent the causes of action can be understood, the "injury" in these causes of action refers only to Vincent Caloiaro, and/or uses the singular "plaintiff" and/or masculine pronouns. Overruled regarding causes of action 4, 5, 6, 7, and 8 alleged against Scott Sidwell. Defendant relies on inapposite authority and has not shown as a matter of law that Defendant Sidwell is not liable. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 =302/MJM

Defendants Scott Sidwell And University Of San Francisco'S Motion To Strike 1st Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Nov-13-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MARCIA A. WALLIS OF THE WALLIS FIRM, TEL. NO. (650) 468-5556 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS;
ATTY. MICHAEL J. VARTARIN, TEL. NO. 391-1155 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendants Scott Sidwell And University Of San Francisco'S Motion To Strike 1st Amended Complaint is Moot in light of ruling on demurrer. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 =302/MJM

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-05-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows:
DEFENDANTS SCOTT SIDWELL AND UNIVERSITY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is continued to November 13 to provide Plaintiffs the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Plaintiffs shall provide a courtesy copy of their opposition to Department 302 no later than November 6 at 4pm, or the demurrer will be sustained, without leave to amend. The Court has courtesy copies of the motion and reply. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Susan Crozier, CSR # 4761 =302/EG
